Pricing

Our prices reflect the competitive value of new and unworn watches, complete with all accessories and a valid manufacturer’s warranty. While we do accept offers to close deals, please understand that our prices are already highly competitive in the market.

Inspection

Before shipping, every watch undergoes a thorough inspection to ensure it matches the description in the listing. We check the timekeeping, case, bracelet, and overall condition, ensuring it’s in perfect order. Each watch is securely packed in its box to ensure safe delivery.

Returns

Buyers have two weeks to inspect their purchase. If the watch is not as described or does not function properly, you may return it for a full refund. Returns will only be accepted if the watch is in the same condition as it was shipped, including all original paperwork and accessories. Watches that have been worn, used, or altered in any way will not be eligible for a return.

Taxes

Sales tax is calculated and added at checkout based on your shipping address, in accordance with state and local laws. Chrono24 collects and remits these taxes directly, and we do not have the ability to waive or alter this amount.

Shipping & Delivery

• United States: All packages are shipped via Overnight shipping and can be delivered to a business address, FedEX office (Hold For Pickup) or a residential address.

• International: We welcome international buyers, and duties/customs fees are the buyer’s responsibility. Certain countries may have shipping restrictions due to insurance limitations, so please contact us to confirm availability.

Why Choose Us?

• Over 24 years of expertise and countless satisfied customers worldwide.
• A commitment to authenticity, transparency, and customer satisfaction.
• Competitive pricing, a 2-year warranty, and secure worldwide shipping.
• Chrono24’s escrow system ensures your funds are secure until your watch is delivered and confirmed.

Few designs in modern watchmaking are as immediately recognizableor as defiantly unconventionalas the Cartier Crash. Born of late-1960s London irreverence and shaped by a spirit of creative rebellion, the Crash remains one of Cartiers most enduring icons. This rose gold example, reference 2463, crafted in the 1990s, captures that lineage in a particularly elegant form. Its softly contorted case, rendered in warm rose gold, flows organically around the wristmore sculpture than instrumentwhile the classic cream dial with elongated Roman numerals stretches and bends within its distorted frame, a study in controlled asymmetry.

The blued steel hands and cabochon-set crown provide the familiar Cartier signatures, grounding the surreal silhouette in the maisons unmistakable design language. Produced in limited numbers and increasingly sought after, 1990s Crash references represent a pivotal chapter in the models historybridging the original London creations with the renewed Paris-era production that reintroduced the design to a new generation of collectors. Ref. 2463 offers the purity of form and proportion that define the Crash at its best: bold, refined, and unapologetically artistic.

Cartier Santos de Cartier Large – ADLC Black Dial (2025) – Full Set

Offered for sale is a Cartier Santos de Cartier Large Model (Ref. WSSA0039) featuring the striking black ADLC-coated stainless steel case and black Roman numeral dial. This modern interpretation of the iconic Santos blends Cartier’s historic design with a sleek, contemporary all-black aesthetic.

This example is a 2025 production piece and remains in excellent pre-owned condition, carefully worn and well maintained. The ADLC coating and case show beautifully with only minimal signs of wear. The watch is running perfectly and keeps excellent time.

The Santos is one of Cartier’s most recognizable models and one of the earliest wristwatch designs in history, originally created for aviator Alberto Santos‑Dumont. This modern ADLC version adds a bold and sporty edge to the classic square case.

The watch includes a complete full set with original Cartier box, papers, and accessories, along with two QuickSwitch straps allowing effortless changes between styles.

Ships priority overnight via Fedex or local to Dallas.

The Original Integrated Sport-Chic

The Santos Carrée reference 2961 is the definitive bridge between Cartier’s early 20th-century aviation roots and the integrated luxury sports watch boom of the late 1970s and 80s. This 1988 example features the classic "square" silhouette with the exposed screws on both the bezel and bracelet—a design language that was intentionally industrial yet inherently sophisticated. The two-tone configuration of stainless steel and 18K yellow gold remains the most iconic look for the Santos, representing the era’s peak aesthetic of versatile, "go-anywhere" luxury.

A Modern Legend With Provenance

Finding a vintage Santos Carrée from the late 80s is one thing; finding one that has retained its original papers for nearly four decades is another. This reference matters because it was the first Santos to utilize an automatic movement, moving the collection away from the quartz trend of the time and back toward serious horology. With its white Roman numeral dial and blued-steel sword hands, it carries the same DNA as the original 1904 design but executed with the robust materials and mechanical reliability required for a modern lifestyle.

Honest Character And Mechanical Health

This piece is in fair overall condition, presenting as an honest "daily driver" that has been worn and enjoyed throughout its life. The steel and 18K gold case and bracelet show visible signs of use and surface wear consistent with a watch of this age, but the structural integrity remains sound. The classic white dial and sapphire crystal are well-preserved. Most importantly, the automatic movement is running great after passing our test bench, showing strong timing. This watch is accompanied by its original Cartier papers, a rare addition that provides significant historical context and peace of mind for the collector.

The Restrained Vintage Essential

This is the watch for the person who wants an iconic Cartier but prefers a piece with a lived-in, vintage feel rather than something that looks brand new. It works because the Santos Carrée is one of the few watches that actually looks better with the character of age. The inclusion of the original paperwork makes it a much more compelling asset than a "naked" example. It is a reliable, stylistically permanent timepiece that serves as the perfect entry point into the world of vintage integrated-bracelet Cartier.

Introducing the Cartier Santos-Dumont WSSA0023, a timepiece inspired by Louis Cartier's historic collaboration with aviator Alberto Santos Dumont in 1904, this watch embodies the spirit of adventure and innovation. This small model boasts a high autonomy quartz movement, ensuring reliable timekeeping for approximately 6 years. The strap, crafted from navy blue alligator leather, adds a touch of sophistication, secured with a steel ardillon buckle.

The Cartier Tank à Guichets WGTA0237 is a striking revival of one of Cartier’s most avant-garde designs, reintroduced in 2025 as a limited edition of 200 pieces. Crafted in platinum, and sized at 37.6 mm x 24.8 mm, the solid case retains the original Art Deco-inspired silhouette, but with a contemporary edge and modern finishing. True to the original 1928 concept, this Tank features no traditional dial—time is revealed through angled jumping hour and minute apertures cut directly into the metal surface, giving the watch its distinctive "guichets" (window) identity.
The brushed platinum façade, free of embellishment, puts full focus on form and function, while the angled aperturessubtly modernize the traditional linear layout. A mechanical manual-winding movement powers the jumping hour complication, maintaining the purist spirit of the original while offering a refined tactile experience. Finished with a black alligator leather strap and platinum folding buckle, the WGTA0237 Tank à Guichets is a bold collector’s piece—fusing Cartier's legacy of mystery and minimalism with rare-case material and a forward-looking design ethos.

Introduced in 1917, the design of the Cartier Tank’s unique case was inspired by the Renault tanks used by the French Army during World War I. Since then, Cartier has released a number of subsidiary lines including the Francaise back in 1996. Incorporating the same extended flanks that have made the original such an icon of watchmaking, the angular lines provide a more contemporary look. This Francaise XL Chrooflex reference incorporates a function-rich quartz calibre, displaying radial date and chronograph complications.

Cartier Santos 4072 is a sophisticated men's watch that features a 40mm stainless steel case, giving it a robust yet stylish appearance. The stainless steel bezel adds to its durability and elegance. The watch has a striking silver dial, complemented by blue steel hands that provide a sharp contrast for easy reading. Black Roman numeral hour markers enhance the classic look, while minute markers encircle the inner dial for precise timekeeping. A date display is conveniently located at the 6 o'clock position, adding functionality to its aesthetic appeal. The bracelet is crafted from stainless steel and includes a secure fold-over clasp with a hidden double push button, ensuring it fits securely on the wrist. It accommodates wrists up to 7.75 inches, offering comfort for daily wear. Protected by a sapphire crystal, the dial is resistant to scratches and wear. The stainless steel case back houses an automatic self-winding movement, which means the watch winds itself with the motion of the wearer's wrist, eliminating the need for batteries. This luxurious timepiece comes with its original box and papers, making it a complete package for any watch enthusiast.

The gorgeous Tank Francaise line emerged in 1996 with some subtle updates to classic Cartier Tank stylings. As such, we believe you will enjoy the dial that is a bit more square than rectangular.

Of course, the elegant silver opaline dial showcases beautiful Roman numerals. In one of the watch’s more endearing moments, the sword-shaped blued steel hands smartly match the dashing blue spinel cabochon upon the octagonal winding crown.

The stainless steel case measures 20 x 25 mm and rests upon a stainless steel bracelet with a hidden butterfly-style folding buckle. Other features include an ergonomic case design and a slightly domed scratch-resistant sapphire crystal.

As a quartz-driven timepiece, we find Cartier’s caliber 057 driving the movement.

Standout features: Elegant silver opaline dial; lovely blued steel hands and Roman numerals; iconic case shape and attractive blue spinel cabochon upon the octagonal winding crown.

Cartier Santos Dumont Extra Large Steel Silver Roman Dial 46.6mm Watch
Ref. WSSA0032
Excellent Condition with Box and Papers. Preowned and only worn a handful of times. Guaranteed 100% authentic.
DESCRITPTION:
This Santos-Dumont watch is the extra-large model. Manufacture mechanical movement with manual winding, caliber 430 MC. Steel case, beaded steel crown set with a blue cabochon synthetic spinel, silver satin-finish dial with sunray-effect, Roman numerals, blued-steel sword-shaped hands, sapphire crystal. Dark blue alligator leather strap, steel ardillon (pin) buckle. Case dimensions: 46.6 mm x 33.9 mm, thickness: 7.5 mm. Water-resistant up to 3 bar (approx. 30 meters/100 feet).
In 1904, Louis Cartier granted the wish of his friend, the famous Brazilian aviator Alberto Santos Dumont to be able to tell the time while flying. This was the birth of one of the first ever wristwatches. Featuring a geometric shape and exposed screws, the watch quickly became one of the Maison's watchmaking icons.

The Cartier Pasha Ref. 2995 is a bold and distinctive timepiece that showcases Cartier’s ability to blend elegance with sporty design. First introduced in the 1980s, the Pasha quickly became a symbol of modern luxury, instantly recognizable by its unique case shape and screw-down crown cap secured with a small chain.

This reference features a 38mm stainless steel case, water-resistant construction, and Cartier’s signature design cues: bold Arabic numerals, luminous sword-shaped hands, and a clean silver dial layout. The unidirectional rotating bezel and screw-down elements add a sporty edge while maintaining Cartier’s refined aesthetic.

Inside beats a reliable automatic movement, ensuring precision and durability for daily wear. The stainless steel bracelet integrates seamlessly with the case, providing comfort and a cohesive, versatile look suitable for both casual and formal occasions.

The Cartier Pasha 2995 remains one of the most distinctive creations in Cartier’s watchmaking history, appreciated by collectors for its blend of style, innovation, and heritage. A true design icon, it offers timeless sophistication with a sporty twist.

Presenting the Cartier Santos-Dumont WSSA0046, a sophisticated tribute to the pioneering spirit of aviator Alberto Santos-Dumont. Crafted by Louis Cartier in 1904 for ease in timekeeping during flight, this timepiece captures Cartier’s design legacy, featuring geometric shapes and exposed screws that make it an enduring icon.

This model is extremely scarce, with very few pieces produced. This Santos-Dumont is powered by Cartier's Calibre 430 MC, a mechanical movement with manual winding, reflecting Cartier's dedication to precision. The elegant steel case showcases black lacquer details, with a steel beaded crown set with a blue synthetic spinel cabochon for a touch of sophistication. Its black square dial with translucent lacquer is adorned with classic Roman numerals and steel sword-shaped hands, protected by sapphire crystal. The case back is engraved with Alberto Santos-Dumont’s signature, further highlighting its exclusive appeal.

Paired with a black alligator-skin strap and steel ardillon buckle, this piece is both comfortable and stylish. The case measures 43.5 mm x 31.4 mm with a slim 7.3 mm thickness, offering water resistance up to 3 bar (approx. 30 meters).


CONDITION: This watch is brand new unworn, direct from an authorized dealer.

Up for grabs is a very rare and immensely popular Cartier Tortue Monopoussoir Chronograph, reference WHTO0008. The Tortue Monopoussoir Chronograph was originally part of Cartier’s CPCP (Collection Prive) line, launched in 1998 to commemorate the 150th anniversary of Cartier. The WHTO0008 stands as a modern heir to the legendary CPCP Tortue chronographs, combining Cartier’s iconic case-shape originally produced in 1912, with a movement designed by watch making legends François-Paul Journe, Denis Flageollet, and Vianney Halter. This particular Cartier Tortue Monopoussoir is housed in platinum, and made in 200 pieces world wide. The watch is sold brand new/unworn, and complete with its original Cartier box and all accompanying paperwork/warranty card.

Powered by a precise quartz movement and protected by a sapphire crystal, the watch is water-resistant to 30 meters, making it as practical as it is beautiful.

The Cartier Pasha was originally created in the 1930s for the Pasha of Marrakesh, a Moroccan ruler who requested a durable, waterproof watch with a crown that could be securely closed. The design featured a round case with a screw-down cap protecting the crown, making it highly functional. The modern Pasha was reintroduced in the 1980s, maintaining its distinctive design elements. Over time, it has become known for its blend of luxury and sporty style, available in various materials and often featuring a square minute track inside the round dial.

About

The Cartier Calibre De Cartier Diver Stainless Steel with a Blue Roman Dial blends refined design with true diving capability. It stands apart as one of Cartier’s few professional dive watches.

The 42mm stainless steel case delivers durability with a polished, elegant profile. Its deep blue dial features Cartier’s signature Roman numerals, offering a distinctive and sophisticated look. Bold sword-shaped hands and luminous markers ensure excellent legibility in low light. The unidirectional rotating bezel provides precise timing for underwater performance.'

Inside, the in-house Cartier Calibre 1904-PS MC automatic movement ensures reliable accuracy and smooth operation. This movement reflects Cartier’s commitment to fine watchmaking and technical innovation. The watch also offers an impressive 300-meter water resistance, making it both functional and luxurious.

The watch appeals to collectors seeking versatility and heritage. It balances sport and elegance without compromise. The Cartier Calibre De Cartier Diver remains a standout choice for those who value performance and iconic design.

The Tank, created by Louis Cartier in 1917, is a model inspired by the bird's-eye view of a tank.
The Tank Normale, which became the catalyst for the release of many Tank variations thereafter, is one of the most iconic designs in watchmaking history.
The Tank Normale "Ref. WGTA0109" made its highly anticipated return in 2023 as the seventh piece in the "Cartier Privé" collection, which has focused on historical timepieces since 2017.
The 18K yellow gold case retains the original's nearly square form measuring 32.6mm x 25.7mm. It features an overall satin finish, complemented by polished edges on the case sides for a sophisticated look.
The distinctive sapphire crystal glass incorporates faceted cuts, creating complex light effects.
Complementing the case finish, the satin-finished silver dial features a traditional railway minute track. The familiar Roman numeral hour markers also conceal a secret sign: 1917, the year the Tank was born.
Cartier crafts captivating timepieces with a design that conveys the diversity and elegance of case forms unmatched by other brands.
Among these, the Tank Normale, an exceptionally significant design, possesses a timeless charm that is never swayed by trends.

An early example of the well-known Santos de Cartier from the late 1980s–90s, this small stainless-steel model is distinguished by its automatic movement and noticeably more robust build than later iterations. The case back is engraved in red with “Santos de Cartier, Automatique, Swiss Made,” underscoring its earlier production period.
The classic Cartier white dial features black Roman numerals, an inner minute track, blued-steel hands, and a sweep seconds hand. It is fitted with the signature Santos screwed steel bracelet, secured by a single deployant clasp embossed with Cartier’s double-C logo.
A clean, timeless Cartier—an ideal classic for everyday wear.

On first glance this looks to be your standard 18k Cartier Tank Louis ref 78086. However, this tank is far more special. What we have here is a reference 88086 made exclusively for the Brazilian market. These tanks were assembled in the Zona Franca de Manaus, a tax-free zone located in the city of Manaus deep in the Amazon on the Rio Negro.

The watch features ZF-Manaus hallmarks on both the case back and solid 18k buckle. It’s preserved in outstanding original condition and features a coveted Paris dial. A rare opportunity to own a classic 18k Tank Louis that has that additional layer of intrigue we all covet.

The signature ivory dial showcases bold black Roman numerals, blue sword-shaped hands, and the hidden Cartier signature at 7 o’clock, offering a nod to the brand's enduring attention to detail. A cabochon-set crown adds a distinctive Cartier flourish, while the watch is completed by a supple leather strap for a look that seamlessly transitions from casual to formal. A perfect choice for collectors or those seeking a sophisticated everyday timepiece with heritage and character.

If you wish to trade in your watch, please send photos and relevant information in your initial message, along with your desired trade value. Our offer for your watch is not binding and is subject to change or revocation. We process all of our trades via insured Fedex Priority Overnight shipping; we ship the watch you are receiving after we receive your watch and the appropriate cleared payment, if a balance is due to us.

Price Suggestions

We may have Price Suggestions enabled on some of our listings; this does not mean we are obligated to negotiate on these listings nor provide price reductions.

Payment

Payment is accepted through Chrono24 Trusted Checkout. We cannot hold watches for longer than 24 hours without proof of payment / payment confirmation. Please submit payment for your watch as soon as your order is confirmed to avoid delays and order cancellation.

All purchases are subject to the sales tax rate of your shipping address.

Returns

We offer a 100% Money Back Guarantee on all of our watches! Feel free to shop with confidence. You have 14 days to return your item. Watches must be in the exact same condition as they were shipped, not sized or altered in any way. If watches are not returned in the same condition, restocking fees may apply.

*We are not an authorized dealer for any of the brands that we list (as are all resellers), however all of our watches are guaranteed authentic.*

Cartier [CAR-TEE-EH]

Founded in 1847 in Paris by Louis-François Cartier as a jewelry store, they designed watches and clocks which were then made for them by other manufacturers. As a son of the original founder, Louis Cartier created several designs for wristwatches which did much to establish the brand’s fame. The Santos and the Tank models, the former created in 1904 and the latter in 1917, were among the first and most iconic wristwatch designs ever produced. These were largely manufactured by the European Watch and Clock Co. (an early formation of Jaeger-LeCoultre). Later, Cartier watches would be made by Tavannes, Wittnauer, Longines, Universal, Patek Philippe, Movado, and ETA. The company remained under family control until 1964, and is currently owned by the Richemont Group

DESCRIPTION
Stunning Green Arabic Dial Like No Other!

The Cartier Santos WSSA0055 commands attention with its rare and striking green Arabic dial — a configuration seldom seen and highly sought after. It merges Cartier’s iconic square case and exposed screw design with a bold, contemporary presence that feels both fresh and timeless.

Versatility defines this reference. It includes two interchangeable straps: a refined green alligator strap and Cartier’s signature rubber strap, both easily swapped via the QuickSwitch system. From formal settings to relaxed weekend wear, this Santos adapts effortlessly while maintaining its distinctive character. The vivid green dial set against polished steel creates a color combination that delivers undeniable wrist presence without excess.

For sale is a pre-owned vintage ladies’ Cartier Tank Louis 18k yellow gold quartz wristwatch, model/reference number 78086. The exact year is unknown, but it is estimated to be from circa the 1980s–1990s. The watch is 100% authentic (the strap is not a Cartier strap) and is running well with no issues. For your added peace of mind, the watch comes with a 1-year warranty through our shop.

The watch has noticeable wear from age and prior use. The dial has paint damage, almost like a “spider dial” effect, though not quite. The blued hands have some debris, and the case has visible surface scratches and scuffs from normal wear over the years. SANTOS DE CARTIER WATCH
1904, Louis Cartier granted the wish of the famous Brazilian aviator Alberto Santos Dumont: to be able to tell time while flying. The birth of one of the first ever wristwatches sealed the bonds of friendship between these two pioneers. The rounded angles of the dial, the seamless curve of the horns, and the exposed screws made for an iconic watch that would inspire countless reinterpretations.

How the Santos Began Its Life - In 1904, Brazilian aviator Alberto Santos-Dumont complained about the unreliability and impracticality of using a pocket watch while flying. Louis Cartier designed a flat wristwatch with a square bezel, which became the first mass-produced men's wristwatch. Santos-Dumont became the first person to be filmed in an airplane flight in 1906, taking 21.5 seconds with a Cartier watch on his wrist. The Santos-Dumont watch was offered in platinum and yellow gold models. Cartier also developed the deployant mechanism for the straps, which is still used today. The Santos continued to be manufactured through the first half of the 20th century, but its square design declined due to WW2 and military requirements for round watches. In 1978, Cartier redesigned the Santos de Cartier, adding an integrated bracelet and stainless steel case and bracelet, a "bimetal" gold-steel design that became popular in the 1980s.

The Cartier Tank has achieved iconic status in the world of watch design and, with the exception of a few distinctive variations, has maintained a consistent aesthetic throughout its extensive production history. The first commercially available Tank Normale was introduced in 1919, having been inspired by the treads of the French Renault FT-17 tank used in WW1. Throughout the decades, Tank watches have been documented on the wrists of notable figures from Jackie Kennedy to Andy Warhol. Today, Cartier feels more relevant in the worlds of fashion, music and popular culture than ever before.
This particular Tank Must is the first of its kind not to be assembled in precious metal. Being cased in steel with a quartz movement, it is a perfect option for daily wear. The blue version offered here is a fun color to add to any lineup. Offered complete with its box and papers, this example remains near mint and is ready to enjoy for decades to come!

The Cartier Pasha Ref. 2995 is a bold and distinctive timepiece that showcases Cartier’s ability to blend elegance with sporty design. First introduced in the 1980s, the Pasha quickly became a symbol of modern luxury, instantly recognizable by its unique case shape and screw-down crown cap secured with a small chain.

This reference features a 38mm stainless steel case, water-resistant construction, and Cartier’s signature design cues: bold Arabic numerals, luminous sword-shaped hands, and a clean silver dial layout. The unidirectional rotating bezel and screw-down elements add a sporty edge while maintaining Cartier’s refined aesthetic.

Inside beats a reliable automatic movement, ensuring precision and durability for daily wear. The stainless steel bracelet integrates seamlessly with the case, providing comfort and a cohesive, versatile look suitable for both casual and formal occasions.

The Cartier Pasha 2995 remains one of the most distinctive creations in Cartier’s watchmaking history, appreciated by collectors for its blend of style, innovation, and heritage. A true design icon, it offers timeless sophistication with a sporty twist.

Cartier [CAR-TEE-EH]

Founded in 1847 in Paris by Louis-François Cartier as a jewelry store, they designed watches and clocks which were then made for them by other manufacturers. As a son of the original founder, Louis Cartier created several designs for wristwatches which did much to establish the brand’s fame. The Santos and the Tank models, the former created in 1904 and the latter in 1917, were among the first and most iconic wristwatch designs ever produced. These were largely manufactured by the European Watch and Clock Co. (an early formation of Jaeger-LeCoultre). Later, Cartier watches would be made by Tavannes, Wittnauer, Longines, Universal, Patek Philippe, Movado, and ETA. The company remained under family control until 1964, and is currently owned by the Richemont Group

This preowned 42mm Cartier Ballon Bleu is stunning. In my opinion, this is the perfect size in the Ballon lineup — elegant yet substantial. The rounded polished steel case and domed sapphire crystal slide effortlessly under a shirt cuff, making it equally at home in a suit or casual wear.

The watch features the Ballon’s signature silver opaline guilloché dial with Roman numeral markers and a date wheel at 3pm. SANTOS DE CARTIER WATCH
1904, Louis Cartier granted the wish of the famous Brazilian aviator Alberto Santos Dumont: to be able to tell time while flying. The birth of one of the first ever wristwatches sealed the bonds of friendship between these two pioneers. The rounded angles of the dial, the seamless curve of the horns, and the exposed screws made for an iconic watch that would inspire countless reinterpretations.
Santos watch, large model, mechanical movement with automatic winding, caliber 1847 MC. Steel case, 7-sided crown set with a synthetic faceted spinel, black dial, black steel sword-shaped hands with luminescent material, sapphire crystal. Steel bracelet with "SmartLink" adjustment system. Second strap in nubuck alligator leather, with interchangeable steel deployant buckle. Both are fitted with the “QuickSwitch” interchangeable system. Case width: 39.8 mm. Thickness: 9.38 mm. Water-resistant up to 10 bar (approx. 100 meters/330 feet).

Cartier Santos 4072 is a sophisticated men's watch that features a 40mm stainless steel case, giving it a robust yet stylish appearance. The stainless steel bezel adds to its durability and elegance. The watch has a striking silver dial, complemented by blue steel hands that provide a sharp contrast for easy reading. Black Roman numeral hour markers enhance the classic look, while minute markers encircle the inner dial for precise timekeeping. The bracelet is crafted from stainless steel and includes a secure fold-over clasp with a hidden double push button, ensuring it fits securely on the wrist. It accommodates wrists up to 7.75 inches, offering comfort for daily wear. Protected by a sapphire crystal, the dial is resistant to scratches and wear. The stainless steel case back houses an automatic self-winding movement, which means the watch winds itself with the motion of the wearer's wrist, eliminating the need for batteries. This luxurious timepiece comes with its original box and papers, making it a complete package for any watch enthusiast.

CARTIER PASHA SEATIMER Ref. 1040 Mens Full Size Watch, Automatic, Date - Stainless Steel

Mint Condition - Fully Serviced - Fabulous Value - 1 Year Warranty

Fashionable and energetic, the Cartier Pasha Seatimer is a distinctive addition to any wardrobe. Complete with unmistakable signs such as the powerfully designed bracelet and screw-down crown coverlet, Cartier states it is, "A real bestseller for people with a strong personality."

According to Cartier, true to its first iteration in 1985, the Cartier Pasha is amplified by originality and uniquely powerful, bringing together elements that go beyond classic watchmaking.

Featuring a very cool diver's elapsed time bezel, a dark gray face with wave textured pattern, classic baton indices, and a convenient date window between four and five o'clock - all protected by a scratch-resistant sapphire crystal - this extremely versatile automatic watch equally complements your favorite business suit, casual attire, or sports outfit.

This is the largest size such Pasha that Cartier made. It measures 38mm in diameter without the crown, and the large capped crown make it wear a bit larger.

We are proud to offer this Certified Pre-Owned Pasha Seatimer watch which presents in gorgeous condition - a 9.7 out of 10. Its case presents as mint with the tiniest spot of aging on the underside near 12 that's only visible under close scrutiny.

Its dial is also like new. Its bracelet presents in gorgeous condition as well.

A Master Watchmaker has fully serviced the handwound Cartier movement. It is running very strong and true, keeping time better than most new Cartiers in our testing recording position.

CARTIER SANTOS-DUMONT BLACK LACQUER - RARE LIMITED PRODUCTION

Reference: WSSA0046 - 2025

DESCRIPTION:
Unworn 2025 Cartier Santos-Dumont in black lacquer, the discontinued variant that will never be made again. Cartier's official statement: "Our Santos-Dumont timepiece (Ref: WSSA0046) was made as a one-shot production with no current reproduction plans." This is the watch that launched wristwatches themselves—Louis Cartier's 1904 creation for Brazilian aviation pioneer Alberto Santos-Dumont—reimagined with striking black lacquer and then discontinued forever. The glossy black dial features Cartier's signature translucent lacquer over engraved square decoration, creating incredible depth and lustrous finish. 43.5mm x 31.4mm stainless steel case with black lacquer is impossibly elegant at just 7.3mm thick. Powered by Cartier Manufacture Caliber 430 MC manual-winding movement. Caseback engraved with Alberto Santos-Dumont's handwritten signature. Steel beaded crown set with synthetic blue spinel cabochon, sapphire crystal, black alligator leather strap with steel ardillon buckle. Factory fresh, still under Cartier manufacturer warranty. Originally retailed at $8,550. Instant collectible that has become one of the most sought-after modern Cartier references.

CONDITION:
Unworn. Factory fresh, never worn on the wrist. Case pristine with protective stickers intact where applicable. Black lacquer with exposed screws flawless. Sapphire crystal perfect. Glossy black lacquer dial immaculate. Caliber 430 MC running perfectly, never used. Black alligator leather strap shows no creasing, no wear. Steel ardillon buckle pristine. Investment-quality unworn example of discontinued reference.

SCOPE OF DELIVERY:
- Cartier Santos-Dumont watch (Ref. WSSA0046)
- Black alligator leather strap with stainless steel ardillon buckle
- Original Cartier inner presentation box
- Original Cartier outer box
- Warranty card dated 2025 - still under manufacturer warranty
- All original Cartier manuals and documentation
- Complete full set - everything as it left the Cartier boutique

Black lacquer variant impossibly rare. Cartier produces very limited pieces and it always gets sold almost immediately. Hurry.

THE TANK À GUICHETS WRISTWATCH
Created by Cartier in 1917, the Tank wristwatch has become an iconic timepiece.

It is the culmination of Louis Cartier's reflections on watchmaking design, initiated at the turn of the 20th century. An aesthete and a visionary, he instigated many changes when he joined his father Alfred at the helm of the Maison in 1898. Louis Cartier urged his jewellers to adopt more geometric, clean-lined forms. He also applied this art of refined simplicity to watchmaking which, according to his vision, should embody the fusion of style and functionality.

After the Santos, introduced in 1904 and described as the first modern wristwatch, a new milestone was reached with the Tank. Its design, which can be best described as pristine simplicity, displays the most essential lines of any timepiece. The case, lugs and bracelet perfectly merge to become one, reflecting a search for pure form. This harmony is especially highlighted by the brancards, two parallel vertical bars which prolong the case's square shape and connect it with the bracelet. This distinctive feature echoed the silhouette of the armoured vehicles that had recently been introduced by the American army on the battlefields of World War I, from which the Tank watch borrowed its name.

A timeless icon, the Tank boasts a strong creative potential which has invited many variations over the decades. First appeared in 1928, the Tankà Guichets (French for hatches) is distinguished by its ultimate sobriety, focusing on the most essential: the time is only indicated by two apertures. The traditional dial disappears, emphasizing the purity of the case with its clean lines and precise proportions. The watch is also characterised by its numerical display, featuring revolving discs. The hour disc was powered by a "jumping" mechanism, the minute disc by a continuous mechanism.

Historically, the production of Tankà Guichets is rare and was mainly concentrated during the late 1920s and 1930s, with one of the first creations acquired by the Maharajah of Patiala and today preserved within the Maison's heritage collection. The Tankà Guichets might also have been a way to answer a specific demand from the then-market, to provide wristwatches with dials that could be protected, especially during sporting activities that were becoming popular.

In 2025, Cartier Privé revisits the Tank à Guichets aesthetic with a platinum model, limited to a numbered edition of 200 pieces. The wristwatch is powered by a hand-wound 9755 MC movement, a calibre with jumping hours and dragging minutes, crafted exclusively for the occasion. The satin-finish case contrasts with the polished horizontal brancards, enhancing the watch's silhouette. The central face is in brushed platinum, unveiling a new disposition: the two apertures are positioned at an angle; while the discreet crown at 12 o'clock pays tribute to the original 1928 timepiece.
Sophistication of the watchmaking technique meets refinement of the design - an emblematic expression of the Cartier style.

UNDERSTATED ELEGANCE
First crafted in 1904, the Santos de Cartier watch embodies minimalism, precise proportions, and meticulous detail. Inspired by the symmetrical geometry of Paris's streets, its iconic square case was specifically designed to be worn on the wrist. Over a century later, it remains a stylish and historically significant piece, beloved by bold individuals who push creative boundaries.

The latest Santos de Cartier collection elevates Cartier's signature clean lines, geometric shapes, and exquisite details. Each watch showcases a decadent selection of dials, finishes, and materials, reflecting Cartier's commitment to elegance and innovation. The timeless appeal of the Santos de Cartier makes it a treasured accessory for those who value both history and modern style.

POLISHED BLACK
The Santos-Dumont watch epitomises the essence of a bold all-black statement piece. Its sleek steel case, coated in deep black lacquer, features iconic details like the square silhouette and signature beaded crown with a blue synthetic spinel cabochon.

The Roman numerals shine through with captivating depth, complemented by steel sword-shaped hands visible under the protective sapphire crystal.

Each watch pays homage to Alberto Santos-Dumont with his engraved signature on the case back. Completing the lustrous look is a black alligator strap secured by a steel ardillon buckle.

The Cartier Tank has achieved iconic status in the world of watch design and, with the exception of a few distinctive variations, has maintained a consistent aesthetic throughout its extensive production history. The first commercially available Tank Normale was introduced in 1919, having been inspired by the treads of the French Renault FT-17 tank used in WW1. Throughout the decades, Tank watches have been documented on the wrists of notable figures from Jackie Kennedy to Andy Warhol. Today, Cartier feels more relevant in the worlds of fashion, music and popular culture than ever before.
This ladies Tank Louis, circa 1970s features a "Swiss" signed dial. This piece has remained in lovely vintage condition. The case retains thick, even lugs and even still has its stock number still visible on the caseback. The dial light aging but nothing distracting and it presents very well to the naked eye. It comes accompanied with a plated Cartier pin buckle. For anyone looking for a well-preserved example, this piece has all the charm one could hope for in a vintage Tank.

Iconic elegance with a dash of playful harmony for good measure—that sums up this lustrous Cartier Panthére Medium Size timepiece for ladies.

The elegance announces itself with poise…note how the 18K yellow gold bezel upon the stainless steel case frames the beauty of the beige-colored dial.

Speaking of the dial, our dash of singular style makes itself apparent if you note the unique date window placement at 5 o’clock. Whether you use it as a conversation piece or a private pleasure, its assured individuality remains.

Along with the blued steel sword-shaped hands we also have Roman numerals upon the hour. Note the classic method of rendering 4 as IIII as opposed to IV. The square-shaped minute track holds the dial together visually and performs a shapely dialog with the 18K yellow gold bezel.

Additional features include a scratch-resistant sapphire crystal, a 20 m / 2 atm water resistance, and a lovely blue sapphire cabochon upon the push / pull winding crown.

The case measures 27 x 27 mm and rests upon a stainless steel bracelet with three rows of yellow gold links and a double-folding hidden buckle.

Standout feature(s): Prominent and gorgeous 18K yellow gold bezel; lovely blue sapphire cabochon upon winding crown; three rows of yellow gold links upon bracelet.

The Cartier Tank has achieved iconic status in the world of watch design and, with the exception of a few distinctive variations, has maintained a consistent aesthetic throughout its extensive production history. The first commercially available Tank Normale was introduced in 1919, having been inspired by the treads of the French Renault FT-17 tank used in WW1. Throughout the decades, Tank watches have been documented on the wrists of notable figures from Jackie Kennedy to Andy Warhol. Today, Cartier feels more relevant in the worlds of fashion, music and popular culture than ever before.
This Tank Louis, circa 1970s features a "Paris" signed dial with hidden "Cartier" signitare at 7 o-clock and the "elongated" cabochon crown which many collectors argue is more attractive. This piece has remained in excellent vintage condition. The displays no cracking or crazing which many examples of this era show today. It has no notable wear. The case retains strong dimensions with hallmarks and caseback markings still clearly visible. It comes accompanied by a supple lizard strap in brown with generic buckle and genuine Cartier service pouch for storage and travel. For anyone looking for a high quality example, we're thrilled to offer you your next dress piece.

Dial: Phenomenally well-preserved for this era with no signs of cracking or crazing which many examples show today. There are no notable areas of wear under magnification.

Case: Retains strong dimensions with thick and even lugs. There is a small dent on the back of the lower left lug - it could easily be polished out. Hallmarks and caseback markings are still present and clearly visible. Correct crown.

Bracelet: Supple two piece lizard strap in brown and generic buckle.

Crystal: Clear.

Accessories: Genuine Cartier service pouch for storage and travel.

This Cartier purse watch was produced in collaboration with Movado and represents a clever intersection of horology and design. Built into a sterling silver case wrapped in lizard leather, the watch is engineered to wind automatically each time the case is opened and closed. This mechanical solution reflects the experimental period of early 20th century watchmaking, when brands like Cartier explored alternative formats beyond the wristwatch. These purse watches were intended as functional jewelry pieces and were often owned alongside traditional wristwatches rather than replacing them.

This is a true cocktail watch in the purest sense. It is discreet, tactile, and designed to be handled rather than worn. The automatic winding mechanism tied to the case action gives it a sense of theater that modern watches rarely offer. For collectors interested in early Cartier oddities or mechanical curiosities, this is the type of piece that quietly stands apart.

The Must de Cartier line, created by Robert Hocq and Alain-Dominique Perrin, aimed to attract new customers by offering accessories and leather goods with the essence of Cartier. The Must de Cartier Tank wristwatch was the brand's most iconic item, made from sterling silver with a minimum of 10k plating at 2.5 microns thick. Launched in 1976, the line marked key milestones for the brand, including being the first non-precious metal watch and the first mass-produced watch. The brand produced around 3000 watches per year before the Must Line, and by the end of the 70s, they were producing over 160,000 watches per year. The dials varied, with faux-stone dials resembling onyx and lapis, lacquer dials in wood and tortoiseshell, and the Trinity dial featuring five stripes in a center section in yellow, white, and rose gold. The Trinity dial was a favorite among fans.

This is a textbook Must Tank. The warm dial tone, crisp Roman numerals, and cabochon crown give it the character people look for in these models. On the wrist, it wears slim and balanced, exactly as a Tank should, and feels more elegant thanks to the manual movement.

The Cartier Tonneau reference 89090016 is a superb expression of early 20th-century horological artistry, echoing the brand's pioneering spirit when it introduced the original Tonneau model in 1906. Crafted in 18K yellow gold, the elegantly curved case measures 39mm by 21mm, with a slender thickness of just 8mm, and perfectly encapsulates the Art Deco aesthetic so coveted among seasoned collectors. The white dial—marked by refined guilloche patterns and blued steel hands—radiates understated luxury, while the mechanical manual-wind movement, in keeping with Cartier’s heritage, provides the tactile engagement prized by connoisseurs. The Tonneau's history stands as a testament to Cartier’s ability to create wristwatches that transcend pure function, serving as style statements with enduring relevance. Released circa the 1990s, this reference appeals to both vintage enthusiasts and those who appreciate timeless, nonconformist design cues. The case curvature ensures an ergonomic fit, while the attention to traditional finishing reflects the maison’s unwavering devotion to detail. Highly sought after but relatively scarce on the secondary market, this Tonneau is esteemed for both its technical refinement and its place in Cartier's lineage of shaped watches. It remains an essential acquisition for serious collectors of the brand or classic dress timepieces. Excellent Condition with Cartier box and papers dated 1996.

Cartier are undoubtedly known for their masterful and unique case designs which in recent years have caught more deserved attention from collectors the world over. The "Fabergé" is a seldom seen model, first appearing in the '70s and named after the famous jeweler to the Russian imperial family: Karl Faberge. This was part of a shift at the time to produce a larger variety of shapes from the Ceinture, Gondole, Cristallor and more. This model makes for a great option for anyone who appreciates Cartier's lesser-known offerings.
This particular example is a larger variant with 26 x 32mm case and pairs well with medium to larger sized wrists. It has remained in stunning vintage condition with a sharp, unpolished case that shows deep, crisp hallmarks and markings. The History of the Cartier Santos
Aviation was still completely new in the early 20th century. Brazilian pilot Alberto Santos-Dumont was especially influential in France due to his experiments with motorized flight. In 1904, Santos-Dumont went to his friend, Louis Cartier, and asked him to create a watch he could use in flight without taking his hands off the controls. Cartier immediately went to work and soon presented the aviation pioneer with the Santos – the world's first genuine men's wristwatch.

In 1906, Santos-Dumont completed the first public flight with a motorized plane. During the flight, which set many world records, Santos-Dumont wore a unique square wristwatch. Demand for the watch quickly increased, leading Cartier to begin series production of the Santos in 1911

The Tank, created by Louis Cartier in 1917, is a model inspired by the bird's-eye view of a tank.
The Tank Normale, which became the catalyst for the release of many Tank variations thereafter, is one of the most iconic designs in watchmaking history.
The Tank Normale "Ref. WGTA0109" made its highly anticipated return in 2023 as the seventh piece in the "Cartier Privé" collection, which has focused on historical timepieces since 2017.
The 18K yellow gold case retains the original's nearly square form measuring 32.6mm x 25.7mm. It features an overall satin finish, complemented by polished edges on the case sides for a sophisticated look.
The distinctive sapphire crystal glass incorporates faceted cuts, creating complex light effects.
Complementing the case finish, the satin-finished silver dial features a traditional railway minute track. The familiar Roman numeral hour markers also conceal a secret sign: 1917, the year the Tank was born.
Cartier crafts captivating timepieces with a design that conveys the diversity and elegance of case forms unmatched by other brands.
Among these, the Tank Normale, an exceptionally significant design, possesses a timeless charm that is never swayed by trends.

Cartier has always been a brand known for its innovation and creativity in case shape and design. In 1904, founder Louis-François Cartier created a watch for his Brazilian pilot friend Alberto Santos-Dumont that could be easily read while flying. This watch was strapped to the wrist and was by many accounts the first wristwatch. Years later, the watchmaker debuted a smaller version of the same watch, calling it the Panthère. Presented on a more delicate high-polished 5 link bracelet and with a slightly more rounded case and bezel, it was a refined version of the original. This Cartier Panthère is featured in 18k yellow gold and has an off-white dial. Cartier’s signature blue cabochon in the crown matches the blue hands. The Panthere also has the traditional Roman numerals found on watches across Cartier’s range. Crown set with a synthetic blue spinel

The Santos Began Its Life - In 1904, Brazilian aviator Alberto Santos-Dumont complained about the unreliability and impracticality of using a pocket watch while flying. Louis Cartier designed a flat wristwatch with a square bezel, which became the first mass-produced men's wristwatch. Santos-Dumont became the first person to be filmed in an airplane flight in 1906, taking 21.5 seconds with a Cartier watch on his wrist. The Santos-Dumont watch was offered in platinum and yellow gold models. Cartier also developed the deployant mechanism for the straps, which is still used today. The Santos continued to be manufactured through the first half of the 20th century, but its square design declined due to WW2 and military requirements for round watches. In 1978, Cartier redesigned the Santos de Cartier, adding an integrated bracelet and stainless steel case and bracelet, a "bimetal" gold-steel design that became popular in the 1980s.

The Cartier Santos Large WSSA0018 refines the iconic square-silhouette design in a polished and brushed 39.8 mm stainless steel case with exposed screws. Its silvered opaline dial features striking black Roman numeral indices and blued-steel sword hands, paired with a date aperture at 6 o’clock—ensuring elegant symmetry and readability. The faceted seven-sided crown set with a blue synthetic spinel adds a subtle signature touch. Wearability is enhanced via Cartier’s SmartLink adjustable steel bracelet and the QuickSwitch system for seamless strap changes, including a secondary calfskin option.
Powered by Cartier’s in-house automatic movement and water-resistant to 100 m, the WSSA0018 balances reliability with restrained refinement. Though not a limited model, its clean aesthetic, modern execution, and strong secondary market momentum make it one of the most desirable Santos variants of recent years

Cartier Santos Large WSSA0071 – Blue Sunburst Dial – Full Set (2025) – Bracelet + Blue Alligator Strap

Offered for sale is a Cartier Santos Large (Ref. WSSA0071) featuring the striking sunburst blue dial. The Santos is one of the most iconic watch designs ever created, originally developed by Cartier in 1904 for aviator Alberto Santos-Dumont and widely considered one of the first purpose-built wristwatches.

This modern Large Santos keeps the classic square case with exposed screws while adding Cartier’s QuickSwitch bracelet system and SmartLink sizing, making bracelet adjustments and strap changes extremely easy. The sunburst blue dial gives the watch a bit more presence compared to the traditional silver dial while maintaining the timeless Santos aesthetic.

The watch comes as a full set and includes the stainless steel bracelet, Cartier blue alligator strap, Cartier buckle, and the Cartier bracelet adjustment tool.

Condition:
Good overall condition. Minor hairlines on the bezel and case, which is very common for the Santos due to the polished surfaces. These are light and hardly noticeable in person.

Early Tank Louis references from the 1970s hold a special place in Cartier’s production history, and this example sits within the first six hundred pieces made for reference 78086. Watches from this early run often show traits that later versions do not, including dial signatures, hand styles, and hallmark practices that reflect the smaller scale of Cartier’s production at the time.

This example features an early Paris signed dial that has aged into a warm cream tone. The finish is consistent across the surface and pairs neatly with the original, very rare gold hands, both of which add to the watch's character without distracting from the classic design. The case remains unpolished with deep hallmarks on the back, including an original store inventory number, a detail collectors appreciate because it documents the watch’s early retail history. The domed crystal also appears original to the watch, matching the profile seen on early mechanical Tanks of this era.

This is the kind of Tank that speaks directly to early Cartier production. The cream aging on the Paris dial, the unpolished case, and the original gold hands give it a presence that modern Tanks cannot replicate. It feels honest, straightforward, and very much in line with what collectors look for in a 1970s Tank Louis.

Every watch is thoroughly vetted, carefully described with complete transparency, and guaranteed authentic. Each piece is offered in honest condition, ensuring collectors receive a timepiece that reflects both originality and integrity.

Introduced in 1917, the design of the Cartier Tank’s unique case was inspired by the Renault tanks used by the French Army during World War I. Since then, Cartier has released a number of subsidiary lines including the Francaise back in 1996. Incorporating the same extended flanks that have made the original such an icon of watchmaking, the angular lines provide a more contemporary look. This Francaise XL Chrooflex reference incorporates a function-rich quartz calibre, displaying radial date and chronograph complications.

The steel case combined with the 18K gold bezel gives the watch a luxurious look, while the brown leather strap with folding clasp ensures comfort and style. Of course, the typical Cartier cabochon stone on the crown is also a must and offers the unmistakable Cartier style.


The white dial provides a beautiful contrast to the Roman numerals and blued hands, while the quartz mechanism ensures precise timekeeping.
